message:
chore(beads): close ft-hfbsp — simd_scan fuzz harness shipped (9c9acfaee)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-ha67e","title":"pattern pack sandbox swallows non-dangling canonicalize failures","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:59:18.467501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:36.519825Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:36.519051Z","close_reason":"Landed in 6a8e698d (fix(patterns): sandbox_resolve_dir propagates canonicalize errors except NotFound)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-hdvvo","title":"[docs][AGENTS.md] sub-crate inventory missing 3 newly-extracted leaf crates (cass-types, caut-types, connector-types)","description":"AGENTS.md:273 claims '10 sub-crates extracted' and lines 279-288 list exactly 10 frankenterm-core-* sub-crates:
  tantivy, ars, fleet, replay, resource-types, error-types, config-types, policy-types, replay-types, telemetry-types

Reality at HEAD 4f53f7a8: 13 sub-crates exist in crates/frankenterm-core-* (verified via 'ls -d crates/frankenterm-core-*'). Three are missing from AGENTS.md:
  - frankenterm-core-cass-types  (extracted in c73c0d13 / ft-8cg6y, 'refactor(cass): extract cass types crate')
  - frankenterm-core-caut-types  (extracted in 0d5cba9f / ft-2z15d, 'refactor(caut): extract caut types crate')
  - frankenterm-core-connector-types (extracted in f06b0e9c / ft-dfd16, 'refactor(core): extract connector telemetry types')

All 3 are listed as workspace members in root Cargo.toml lines 11-13. ft-f1ec3 closure on 2026-04-26 explicitly stated AGENTS.md was refreshed and listed all 10 sub-crates with bead pointers + LOC counts; 3 more landed in the next 24h without doc updates.

Suggested fix: append rows to the AGENTS.md sub-crate tree at lines 279-288 with the new bead pointers (ft-8cg6y, ft-2z15d, ft-dfd16) and bump the count from '10 sub-crates extracted' to '13'. Same drift applies to README.md if it cites the same number.

Repro:
  ls -d crates/frankenterm-core-* | wc -l   # → 13
  grep -c 'frankenterm-core-' AGENTS.md      # significantly fewer than 13 entries in the inventory tree","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"amber-fox","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:51.637163Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:53:03.670505Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:53:03.669905Z","close_reason":"Shipped a6e0c2cd: AGENTS.md sub-crate inventory updated to list cass-types (ft-8cg6y, ~870 LOC), caut-types (ft-2z15d, ~458 LOC), connector-types (ft-dfd16, ~273 LOC); count comment replaced with shell command so future drift surfaces immediately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-hdvvo","depends_on_id":"ft-f1ec3","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:56.132625Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-he0w7","title":"[fuzz] add native_events::decode_wire_event fuzz target for socket wire protocol","description":"Surface: crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs:521 — fn decode_wire_event(line: &str) -> Result<Option<NativeEvent>, String>. Parses newline-delimited JSON wire events from the wezterm event socket (WireEvent::{Hello,PaneOutput,StateChange,UserVar,PaneCreated,...}). The PaneOutput variant embeds a base64-encoded data_b64 payload that becomes a Vec<u8> bounded by MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES. No existing fuzz target covers this protocol boundary.

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/native_events_wire.rs
  #![no_main]
  use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
  fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
      if data.len() > 64_000 { return; }
      let Ok(line) = std::str::from_utf8(data) else { return; };
      // decode_wire_event is private. Expose via pub(crate) or a
      // test-only re-export, OR target the nearest public entry point
      // (event_stream::try_decode_line if it exists, otherwise add
      //  gated behind a 
      // feature).
      let _ = frankenterm_core::native_events::decode_wire_event_for_fuzz(line);
  });

Corpus seeds: fuzz/corpus/native_events_wire/ — one file per WireEvent variant with realistic ts/pane_id values, plus malformed-base64 PaneOutput, oversized data_b64 (>MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES to hit truncation), empty line, non-UTF-8-in-JSON-string, deeply-nested escape sequences.

Changes required: add pub(crate) fn decode_wire_event_for_fuzz in native_events.rs behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = "fuzz"))].","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:18.642190Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:19:00.447094Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:19:00.446086Z","close_reason":"Fixed in ea93a349","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-hfbsp","title":"[fuzzing] simd_scan::scan_newlines_and_ansi: SIMD bytes path on every captured pane delta with no fuzz target","description":"## Attack surface

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/simd_scan.rs` contains the SIMD-
optimized scanner that runs against every byte of pane output ft
ingests:

```rust
pub fn scan_newlines_and_ansi(bytes: &[u8]) -> OutputScanMetrics    // line 194
pub fn logical_line_count(self, bytes: &[u8]) -> usize              // line 25 (impl method)
```

Existing fuzz coverage:
- `fuzz/fuzz_targets/scan_pipeline_chunked.rs` (8.1 KB) — drives
  the full scan pipeline including `process(bytes)`, but at the
  pipeline level not the SIMD primitive.
- `fuzz/fuzz_targets/scan_pipeline_quick.rs` (458 B) — drives
  `quick_scan(bytes)` which is the public bucket-counting path.

Neither calls `scan_newlines_and_ansi` directly. The pipeline
fuzzers exercise it transitively but the strongest coverage —
direct fuzz of the SIMD core with raw byte input — is missing.

## Why fuzzing finds real defects here

SIMD code is a known panic + UB hotspot:

- **Out-of-bounds read on chunk-tail handling** — SIMD scanners
  typically vectorize 16/32/64-byte chunks then handle the tail
  in a scalar loop. An off-by-one in the tail loop is a classic
  UB that ASan would catch immediately.
- **Integer overflow in ANSI parameter accumulator** — ANSI
  sequences contain numeric parameters; an attacker pane outputting
  `\x1b[99999999999999999m` could overflow the 32-bit accumulator
  before the bounds check.
- **Alignment crash on architectures without unaligned vector loads**
  — macOS arm64 tolerates unaligned, but the `simd_scan` may have
  fallback paths for x86 with stricter requirements.
- **Quadratic blowup on adversarial patterns** — `\x1b[\x1b[\x1b[`
  recursive escape introducer prefixes can drive worst-case scan
  cost.

This code runs against every pane delta — bytes from agent
output, which IS untrusted (a malicious agent process could emit
crafted bytes). Every byte of every captured agent goes through
this function.

## Suggested implementation

`fuzz/fuzz_targets/simd_scan_bytes.rs`:

```rust
#![no_main]
use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
use frankenterm_core::simd_scan::scan_newlines_and_ansi;

fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
    if data.len() > 16 * 1024 * 1024 { return; }

    // Crash detector
    let metrics = scan_newlines_and_ansi(data);

    // Invariant: counts are bounded by length
    assert!(metrics.newlines as usize <= data.len(),
        "newlines count exceeds input length");
    assert!(metrics.ansi_sequences as usize <= data.len(),
        "ansi sequences count exceeds input length");
});
```

Seed corpus:
- `samples/simd_seed/empty` — empty input
- `samples/simd_seed/just_newlines` — `\n` * 1024
- `samples/simd_seed/just_ansi` — `\x1b[m` * 256
- `samples/simd_seed/realistic_terminal` — 4KB of real `vim`/`htop`
  output (already in the corpus tree under `tests/corpus/`)
- `samples/simd_seed/long_param` — `\x1b[` + `9` * 1024 + `m`
- `samples/simd_seed/recursive_csi_intros` — `\x1b[\x1b[\x1b[\x1b[\x1b[m`

Bonus: differential against a scalar reference impl (Pattern 3)
to catch SIMD/scalar divergences. The `simd_scan` module
likely already has a fallback scalar path under `#[cfg(not(target_feature))]`.

## Out of scope

- The `pattern_detect_with_context_streaming` fuzzer at the
  detection layer (different surface).
- Optimizing the SIMD code itself.

## Acceptance criteria

- `fuzz/fuzz_targets/simd_scan_bytes.rs` exists + registered.
- Seed corpus has ≥6 diverse entries.
- 24h ASan+UBSan campaign produces zero unique crashes.
- Differential variant (if scalar fallback exists) produces zero
  divergences.
- Crashes (when found) convert to regression tests under
  `tests/simd_scan_fuzz_regressions.rs`.

## References

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/simd_scan.rs:25, 194`
- Skill: testing-fuzzing Hard Rule #5 ("ASan without UBSan is half
  a tool" — UBSan catches the integer-overflow class for ANSI
  parameter accumulators)

Filed by cc_1 fuzzing audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:36:44.482925Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T10:52:48.230972Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Filed by cc_1 fuzzing audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:36:44.482925Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T10:58:31.795681Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T10:58:31.795433Z","close_reason":"simd_scan fuzz harness shipped at 9c9acfaee. The harness file (previously untracked from earlier agent work) is now committed with material enhancements: (1) added multi-split state-stitching invariant — original single-split coverage PLUS up to 8 chunk boundaries per input via Arbitrary-driven multi_split_seeds, ANSI state machine carried forward across all chunks via one OutputScanState (matches production streaming model). (2) seeded fuzz/corpus/simd_scan/ with 10 adversarial inputs (csi_basic, osc_then_csi, dcs_st_terminator, recursive_csi_intro, integer_overflow_param, long_osc_payload, plain_newlines, empty, lone_esc, csi_intro_only). Four contracts pinned: SIMD vs scalar reference equivalence, logical_line_count consistency, ansi_density finite+bounded, multi-chunk stitching equivalence. cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin simd_scan → exit 0. Long-running campaign (cargo +nightly fuzz run simd_scan -- -runs=5000) is the deployment step; harness compiles clean and is registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml. Mirrors ft-h8v8v's Archetype 5 + Archetype 1 shape, with SIMD/scalar differential as the primary crash-detector oracle.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-hg6io","title":"[security][follow-up ft-h90rh] Wire mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation into record_policy_denial_audit (call-site + policy.rs helper + test)","description":"## Context

ft-h90rh storage-side infra shipped in 4595cc0e: policy_denied_audit table + StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit async method are live. What's still missing to close the observability loop end-to-end:

1. A convenience wrapper in crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs (e.g. `fn record_policy_denial(storage: &StorageHandle, decision: &PolicyDecision, tool_name: &str, agent_id: Option<&str>, intent_hash: Option<&str>) -> Result<()>`) that builds the PolicyDeniedAuditRecord from a PolicyDecision and writes it. Skipped in the infra ship because policy.rs had an active +81/-16 uncommitted diff from pane 2's ft-eu0no rate-limiter refactor.
2. Wire the wrapper into crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs::mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation alongside the existing tracing::warn!. Challenge: the gate helper is sync; record_policy_denial_audit is async. Options:
   - (a) Gate helper returns a GateOutcome enum carrying the PolicyDeniedAuditRecord; each of the 5 callers (tx_run/rollback, mission_pause/resume/abort) — which already live inside runtime.block_on scopes — writes the row in its deny branch.
   - (b) Fire-and-forget spawn inside the sync gate via runtime_compat::spawn + block_on(None) pattern, dropping the Result.
   (a) is cleaner and per-caller controllable; (b) is smaller diff but loses errors.
3. A round-trip integration test: open a fresh DB, run migration, call record_policy_denial_audit with a known record, query it back, assert equality. Bonus: a per-tool deny-policy end-to-end test that exercises the full mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation → audit-row pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A denied wa.tx_run (or any of the 5 gated tools) under a deny policy produces both a tracing::warn! emission AND a row in policy_denied_audit queryable via SELECT.
- Integration test covering the round-trip.
- policy.rs helper has at least one unit test that builds a record from a synthetic PolicyDecision.

## Scope note

Requires mcp_tools.rs reservation and a clean-enough policy.rs to avoid collision with ft-eu0no. Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-h90rh infra, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:43:12.398456Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:00.875781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:00.875472Z","close_reason":"Partial ship in 98b8f3d4 — docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md with every authorize call site in mcp_tools.rs enumerated (12 distinct deny paths total), current deny handling per site, and a sequenced wiring plan. Actual wiring deferred: policy.rs had +81/-16 uncommitted from pane 2's ft-eu0no SharedRateLimiter refactor (PolicyEngine.rate_limiter field type change) and mcp_tools.rs had +70 uncommitted including the 6x duplicated let policy_rate_limiter cluster flagged in 810ad4c9. Shipping partial wiring under ft-hg6io would have bundled pane 2's in-flight work and likely not compiled standalone. Filed ft-rsqap as the blocked-on-ft-eu0no follow-up that picks up the matrix and actually wires the 12 deny paths once the file contention clears.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","follow-up","mcp","policy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-hph8i","title":"[ft-gkqej/1] Backfill swept_at_sha + sweep_paths + stale_after_commits frontmatter on existing sweep markers","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:57:57.159188Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:28:44.178298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:28:44.178057Z","close_reason":"Backfilled SHA-anchored frontmatter on the three existing audit-sweep markers in /Users/jemanuel/.claude/projects/-Users-jemanuel-projects-frankenterm/memory/. Each marker now carries swept_at_sha (the last commit on the swept paths before the 2026-04-20 sweep), sweep_paths (canonical path list), stale_after_commits (per-subsystem threshold reflecting churn rate), and findings_summary per the spec at docs/proposals/audit-marker-staleness.md. SHAs found via 'git log --before=2026-04-21 --after=2026-04-19 -- <paths>': patterns 2ca20e18 (threshold 5), storage f5a37ec9 (threshold 30 — fast-churn subsystem with 179 commits in 30d at sweep time), mux/term/codec 312f06b9 (threshold 20). MEMORY.md index entries updated to reflect that all three markers are NOW STALE per their own thresholds — verified via git rev-list --count <sha>..HEAD: patterns 24>5, storage 154>30, mux+term+codec 91>20. The staleness data is now actionable; ft-nedq3 (audit_marker_doctor helper) can compute STALE verdicts directly. No code changes; pure docs/metadata work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","memory","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-hph8i","depends_on_id":"ft-gkqej","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:58:10.620399Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-hsmjb","title":"Event::PaneDisappeared declared but never emitted from production runtime","description":"events.rs:181 declared Event::PaneDisappeared. event_stream filters, wire_protocol serializer, main.rs Web SSE route, and tests all matched on it — but no production code path in runtime.rs::handle_native_event ever called bus.publish(PaneDisappeared). Consumers remained dormant forever. Fix landed in 1f3ae436: publish on NativeEvent::PaneDestroyed guarded on event_bus.is_some(). Tests landed in 41cab3bb: ft_pp7jk_pane_destroyed_publishes_pane_disappeared_event + companion no-bus path. Symmetrical to Event::PaneDiscovered which was already published. Unlocks future long-lived subsystems (policy caches, connector bridges) that want per-pane state teardown.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:33.083454Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:24.250654Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:24.250393Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1f3ae436 (fix(runtime): publish Event::PaneDisappeared from handle_native_event) + 41cab3bb (tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
